name: Mashed Potatoes with Garlic (Grausman)
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the potatoes and cut them into large chunks. Place the chunks in a bowl of cold water until ready to cook.
2. In a 4-quart pot, place the potato chunks and garlic. Add enough cold water to barely cover the potatoes (the tips of several potatoes will not be covered).
3. Add 1/2 teaspoon salt to the water and bring it to a boil over high heat.
4. Reduce the heat to medium-high and simmer, uncovered, for 20 to 30 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender. The potatoes are done when a knife inserted into them goes in and out easily without potato clinging to the knife.
5. Drain the potatoes well.
6. Using a potato masher, food mill, or similar device, mash the potatoes until smooth. Avoid using a food processor, as this can make the mixture too pasty.
7. If needed, add some of the cooking liquid (or milk) to achieve the desired consistency.
8. Season with the remaining salt and pepper.
[per serving: Calories, 195; protein, 5 grams; carbohydrates, 44 grams; fat, 0.3 gram; cholesterol, none; sodium, 233 milligrams]
